text:
that the person appearing to the court to have care and control of [name of child], who is under the age of 16, is [the local authority] / [name];

that such tests be carried out by [accredited company name];

the results of such tests shall be filed at court and served on the parties by 4.00pm on [date]; and

the costs of such testing shall be paid by [name] / [the parties equally].

(if testing is through cafcass/cafcass cymru)

An issue around parentage has been raised in connection to a Child Arrangements order which the court needs a report to ascertain whether [putative father’s name] is the father of [name of child].

A tester needs samples from [putative father’s name] and [name of child] so that they can carry out their tests and prepare a report.

The court officer must complete Parts I and II of the standard court form BD1 and send this to [(if testing is through cafcass) DNArequests@cafcass.gov.uk and inboundpost@cafcass.gov.uk] / [(if testing is through cafcass cymru) CafcasscymruCAT@gov.wales].

A [Cafcass] / [CAFCASS Cymru] contracted officer must then make arrangements with [putative father’s name] and [name of person with care and control of child] for mouth samples to be given by [name of child] and [putative father’s name] supervised by an officer commissioned by [Cafcass] / [CAFCASS Cymru].

[Putative father’s name] and [name of person with care and control of child] must allow the officer of [Cafcass] / [CAFCASS Cymru] to take photographic identity of their identity at the time the sample is taken.

The [Cafcass] / [CAFCASS Cymru] contracted officer must send the samples to be tested to arrive by no later than date roughly 6 weeks from the date a valid order and BD1 is sent to Cafcass.

The tester must send the written test report to the courts and the courts only by date roughly 6 weeks from the date the valid DNA order and BD1 is sent to [Cafcass] / [CAFCASS Cymru].
